reviews.comment: My suggestion for Scott is to engage in anti-white supremacy culture training, because he's certainly one. Special topics include only one right way, paternalism, power hoarding, fear of open conflict, etc.
He wanna be woke 24/7. His thesis is that we all have biases toward Blacks & women & should work on our "unconscious biases." He forgot the fact that he was hired to teach computer science.

He doesn't get in the way of your learning. Homework makes sense. Can't go wrong with him. In CSC226, you learn a lot without expending an unreasonable amount of effort. Gave me the creeps the first month of the semester for some reason, no idea why.

Scott is an "inclusiveness" guy. Read to do implicit bias training. Jan is "gender equality" and "employment" girl. Mario is anything in between. One thing nobody ever does is what they're hired for: to teach computer science. Scott is the best among those, I must say, however.
Scott is one of the best professor currently employed at Berea. I don't agree with the person who suggested to avoid him. He is a very friendly and understanding person. Definitely recommend!

Scott is the worst professor currently employed at Berea. It doesn't seem as though he knows the first thing about teaching. I got a high grade in the class, but I felt as though I learned nothing despite all of the long hours of homework I had to do. Do yourself a favor and avoid this guy.
Gave us long coding assignments to teach us about easy concepts. Made everything harder than it had to be. weekly quizzes and homework. decent professor.
He is just amazing. He is the reason  I am pursuing CS.
He quizzes you almost everyday. He makes the midterm seem so easy then grades super hard. He expects so much out of you even if you've never animated before. Overall, he's a good guy though.

Scott is really friendly and laid-back. His animation class is amazing. In that class, he does not lecture a lot because, you know, it's computer science. Most of the things are learned through practice. We used a program called Alice. It's not the easiest program to use, but it will help you to grasp the basics of computer science concepts.

His lectures are always very clear and engaging. I enjoyed his class so much that I went to his class even when I could skip two days or so for the semester. He is very supportive and understanding, and he will go out of way to help students. I learned a lot from his class, and his class confirmed my decision to major in computer science.
